tutorial.title: Getting started
tutorial.description: New to Smart Agent? Take a quick tour to learn what you can do.
tutorial.step0Desc: Smart Agent provides identity verification for AI agents. Pay-per-verification via x402 protocol on Avalanche. 14+ government database integrations, ERC-8004 agent standard.
tutorial.step0Tip: You can use credits or x402 (crypto) to pay for validations.
tutorial.step1Desc: Experience the x402 flow natively. The agent identifies paid endpoints, requests authorization via wallet signature, and executes verification on-chain. A complete payment-for-truth loop.
tutorial.step2Desc: Test our endpoints directly from your browser. Explore every capability, customize parameters, and see real-time responses with a single click.
tutorial.step3Desc: Need to validate millions of users? Upload CSV or Excel files. Our agent processes records in parallel, cleaning your database and enriching it with verified official government data.
tutorial.step3Tip: Supports large files and multiple columns for batch validation.
tutorial.step4Desc: Smart Scan for document extraction, Status Check for API health monitoring, Incidents for service alerts, and Smart Reduce for image optimization.
tutorial.step5Desc: Manage your subscription and add credits. Pay-per-verification with no fixed subscription required.

login_methods.title: Verification process
login_methods.subtitle: Define and configure what type of processes the user can use to authenticate within your project.
login_methods.email_subtitle: This process allows users to seamlessly access their accounts by simply entering their email address. A one-time password (OTP) will then be immediately sent to the email address provided, enhancing authentication security effortlessly.
login_methods.phone_subtitle: This process enables users to log in by receiving a one-time password (OTP) sent to the mobile phone number they provide.
login_methods.phone_recommendation: You must select at least one OTP shipping method. If you wish, you can activate both SMS and WhatsApp sending.
login_methods.face_liveness_subtitle: Allow users to authenticate themselves using a biometric registration.
login_methods.face_recommend_subtitle: When enabled, users with uncertain selfie quality will see a suggestion to re-register their face.
login_methods.allow_passkeys_subtitle: Let users sign in with platform passkeys (Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello) as an additional factor.
login_methods.liveness_subtitle: Allow users to verify their presence and liveness using a biometric registration.
login_methods.liveness_recommendation: We recommend a score of 60 for it to work optimally on all devices.
login_methods.search_subtitle: A higher search score (1N) requires a high fidelity resemblance in order to grant access to the user.
login_methods.search_recommendation: We recommend a score of 85 to ensure optimal performance.
login_methods.additional_subtitle: Fine-tune secondary behaviours for the login experience.
login_methods.at_least_one_method: Enable at least one authentication method (email, phone, or biometrics) to continue.

hub.title: Demos
hub.metaDescription: Authentication anchored to human presence. Run Verifik's live biometric demos for face match, liveness, and HumanID in the browser.
hub.heroBody: Run face match, liveness, HumanID, and live detection in the browser. Centralized biometrics cover server-side face gallery management and search. Decentralized HumanAuthn demos show how a HumanID binds identity data to a live face without a central face database.
hub.enterpriseBody: Our platform processes biometric data with end-to-end encryption. Privacy by design, ready for serious deployments.

create-collection.title: Create Collection
create-collection.description: Create a named face collection to group enrolled persons for search and verification.

create-person.title: Create Person
create-person.description: Enroll a new person by uploading face images and metadata. Stored in your centralized face gallery.

create-person-with-liveness.title: Create Person with Liveness
create-person-with-liveness.description: Enroll a person while simultaneously running a liveness check and searching the collection for duplicates.

update-person.title: Update Person
update-person.description: Load a person by id and change profile fields or collection membership (no new face images in this API).

delete-person.title: Delete Person
delete-person.description: Delete a person completely or remove them from a single collection when they belong to several.

search-person.title: Search Person
search-person.description: Upload one or more face images and search the collection for matching persons by similarity score.

search-live-person.title: Search Live Person
search-live-person.description: Capture a live face, run liveness detection, and search the collection for a match in one flow.

search-active-user.title: Search Active User
search-active-user.description: Authenticate an active user with a live face capture and return a liveness score alongside search results.

detect-face.title: Detect Face
detect-face.description: Submit an image and detect all faces it contains, returning bounding boxes and crop data.

search-crops.title: Search Crops
search-crops.description: Run a multi-image crop search to find matching persons across uploaded face crops with precision tuning.

face-comparison.title: Face Comparison
face-comparison.description: Upload two photos and see if they are the same person. Useful for onboarding and ID checks.

face-comparison-liveness.title: Compare with Liveness
face-comparison-liveness.description: Match a reference photo to a live selfie. Liveness runs only after the face match looks strong enough.

verify-face.title: Verify Face
verify-face.description: Verify a face image against a specific enrolled person by ID with a configurable similarity threshold.

liveness.title: Liveness Detection
liveness.description: Show that a real person is in front of the camera, not a photo, mask, or replay.

humanid.title: HumanID
humanid.description: Capture a live face and authenticate with a decentralized HumanID proof. No central face database required.

humanid-create.title: Create HumanID

faceComparison.apiRefBody: Verifik's public pages may describe extra fields for other environments. This HumanAuthn demo calls the Verifik route that accepts gallery, probe, and optional compare_min_score only.

faceComparison.bulletShortB64: Images must be real base64 payloads. Very short strings are rejected (for example 412:only_images_in_base64).
faceComparison.bulletSlider: The Compare minimum score slider sets compare_min_score on the request (0.67 to 0.95; starts at the API client default {{defaultScore}}).
faceComparison.bulletResponseShape: Responses often include id and a signature block alongside data.
faceComparison.fallbackMatch: The two faces belong to the same person.
faceComparison.fallbackNoMatch: The faces appear to be different individuals.

faceComparisonLiveness.sourcePara1: Source is sent as gallery for face comparison.
faceComparisonLiveness.sourcePara2: Liveness does not use Source. It runs on Target in the next step.
faceComparisonLiveness.targetPara1: This Target image is the probe.
faceComparisonLiveness.targetPara2: If compare passes your threshold, liveness runs on this image only. Source is not used for liveness.
faceComparisonLiveness.chooseOneDescription: Scan with your camera or upload a photo. Either option works for this step.
faceComparisonLiveness.cameraSubtitleTarget: Capture the target image with liveness
faceComparisonLiveness.uploadSubtitle: Select an image file from your device instead
faceComparisonLiveness.uploadSecondary: JPEG or PNG from your gallery
faceComparisonLiveness.cropCheckboxTitle: Crop my Source photo to the detected face
faceComparisonLiveness.cropCheckboxBody: When on, we detect the largest face in your first image (Source, step 1) and replace it with a cropped region around that face before we send it as gallery. Your second image (Target, step 2) is sent as you captured it, with no crop in this demo. Use this for group shots, ID scans, or busy frames where the face is small or off center.
faceComparisonLiveness.cropErrorNoFace: We couldn’t detect a face to crop in your Source photo (step 1). Try a clearer picture, or turn off Source face crop and try again.
faceComparisonLiveness.cropErrorGeneric: We couldn’t crop your Source photo. Try another image, or turn off Source face crop.
faceComparisonLiveness.apiRefEndpointDesc: Compare first; if the face score meets compare_min_score (or it is omitted), liveness runs on the probe image against liveness_min_score.
faceComparisonLiveness.apiRefProbeGallery: In this demo, Source is the gallery image (step 1). Target is the probe (step 2). You can optionally detect the largest face in the Source and crop the image to that region in the browser before sending. The Target image is not cropped here. Server integrations may also support cropping via cropFace.
faceComparisonLiveness.paramGalleryDesc: Reference images (base64; https URLs supported server-side)
faceComparisonLiveness.bulletSeq: Comparison runs first; liveness on probe only if compare passes your threshold (or threshold omitted).
faceComparisonLiveness.bulletLivenessDefault: liveness_min_score defaults to 0.6 when not sent.
faceComparisonLiveness.bulletGalleryUrl: Gallery may use https URLs; the service fetches and converts them.
faceComparisonLiveness.msgVerified: Compare passed your threshold and probe liveness passed.
faceComparisonLiveness.msgBelowSkip: Face similarity is below compare_min_score, so liveness was not executed.
faceComparisonLiveness.msgBelowMatch: Face similarity is below the compare threshold.
faceComparisonLiveness.msgLivenessLow: Probe liveness score is below the configured minimum.
faceComparisonLiveness.engineBlock:
ENGINE: HumanAuthn
PROTO: CWL_SEQ
AUTH: PENDING

humanidCreate.apiIntro: Bind public identity data and a face biometric into an encrypted ZelfProof stored on IPFS. Only the matching live face can decrypt it.
humanidCreate.heroSubtitle: Bind identity data + face into an encrypted ZelfProof on IPFS.
humanidCreate.step1Subtitle: Who this HumanID belongs to—public fields on the proof and private data that stays encrypted until you decrypt.
humanidCreate.step2Subtitle: Anchor the proof to a live face.
humanidCreate.step3Subtitle: Choose when a live face is required — at encryption (creation), at decryption (unlock), or both. Tolerance applies whenever liveness is enabled.
humanidCreate.identifierHint: A unique handle for this HumanID — usually a username, email, or document number.
humanidCreate.livenessOnEncryptTitle: Require a live face when creating
humanidCreate.livenessOnEncryptHint: When ON, the face photo used to create this HumanID must pass a liveness (anti-spoof) check at encryption time. Use this to block static photos or prints during creation.
humanidCreate.livenessOnDecryptTitle: Require a live face when decrypting
humanidCreate.livenessOnDecryptHint: When ON, every decrypt must use a real-time camera capture (anti-spoof). When OFF, a still photo of the same person can unlock the proof — easier for users, less secure.
humanidCreate.livenessSettingsIntro: These are independent settings. You can require liveness only at creation, only at decrypt, both, or neither. Decrypt liveness also enables monthly active-user billing.
humanidCreate.livenessSettingsSectionSubtitle: Anti-spoof checks — separate controls for create vs unlock.
humanidCreate.unlockToleranceHint: How strict anti-spoof (liveness) checks should be whenever a live face is required at encryption or decrypt.
humanidCreate.toleranceRegularDescription: More permissive; high-quality photos may pass as “live”. Prefer Hardened when you need to block static images.
humanidCreate.toleranceSoftDescription: More lenient liveness — fewer failed checks, slightly higher spoof risk.
humanidCreate.toleranceHardenedDescription: Strictest liveness — strongest anti-spoof, may reject some genuine captures.
humanidCreate.unlockPasswordHint: Adds a second factor on top of the face. Leave blank for face-only decryption.
humanidCreate.publicDataHint: Readable with the proof without decrypting; all values are sent as strings.
humanidCreate.metadataHint: Secret information encrypted in the proof. Only your matching face can decrypt and reveal these values later. All values are sent as strings.
humanidCreate.chooseOneDescription: Scan with your camera or upload a photo. Either option works for this step.
humanidCreate.cameraSubtitle: Open the camera and align your face in the frame
humanidCreate.uploadSubtitle: Select an image file from your device instead
humanidCreate.errorPublicDataJson: Public data: fix the JSON (invalid or incomplete), then try again.
humanidCreate.errorMetadataJson: Private data: fix the JSON (invalid or incomplete), then try again.
humanidCreate.errorKeysRequired: Public data and private data need at least one key with a non-empty key name.
humanidCreate.errorLivenessFailedBody: This image did not pass liveness verification for HumanID. Use the guided camera or another real-time capture, then try again.
humanidCreate.paramMetadata: Private key-value object encrypted in the proof; revealed only when you decrypt with your face
humanidCreate.paramTolerance: Liveness strictness when live face is required: REGULAR / SOFT / HARDENED (also REGULAR_HARD, REGULAR_SOFT upstream)

humanidCreateQr.errorLivenessFailedBody: This image did not pass liveness verification for HumanID. Use the guided camera or another real-time capture, then try again.
humanidCreateQr.paramMetadata: Private key-value object encrypted in the proof; revealed only when you decrypt with your face
humanidCreateQr.paramTolerance: Liveness strictness when live face is required: REGULAR / SOFT / HARDENED (also REGULAR_HARD, REGULAR_SOFT upstream)

humanidDecrypt.heroSubtitle: Recover identity data by presenting the matching live face.
humanidDecrypt.qrUploadHelp: Upload a PNG or JPEG of the HumanID QR (binary payload). We call preview-zelf-id-qr to extract your HumanID.
humanidDecrypt.errorReadProof: Could not read HumanID from preview response.
humanidDecrypt.qrExtractSuccess: HumanID extracted from QR. You can decrypt below.
humanidDecrypt.passwordPlaceholder: Only if the proof was created with a password
humanidDecrypt.chooseOneDescription: Scan with your camera or upload a photo. Either option works for this step.
humanidDecrypt.cameraSubtitle: Open the camera and align your face in the frame
humanidDecrypt.uploadSubtitle: Select an image file from your device instead
humanidDecrypt.apiOfficialParen: (request and response fields, optional password and verifier key, and example calls).
humanidDecrypt.apiBaseUrlNote: Base URL https://api.verifik.co. Send JSON with Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Bearer <access token> (same JWT as this demo after sign in).
humanidDecrypt.apiDecryptEndpointDesc: Compares your face image to the proof, then returns decrypted public fields and related payload (for example face crop and difficulty) inside the usual signed data envelope when the match succeeds.
humanidDecrypt.apiPreviewQrEndpointDesc: Use this when the user provides a PNG or JPEG of the HumanID QR. The API reads the QR payload, runs the same kind of preview as a string preview, and returns a zelfProof you can pass into decrypt. Optional verifierKey applies when the proof was created with one. Same JSON headers as decrypt.
humanidDecrypt.apiBullet1: Decrypt uses decryptHumanId from @humanauthn/api-client. QR upload uses previewZelfIdQr first, then you decrypt with the returned proof string.
humanidDecrypt.apiBullet2: Success responses follow the Verifik pattern: a data object (and often a signature when signing is enabled for your project).
humanidDecrypt.apiBullet3: To inspect a proof without a face image, open the Preview HumanID demo (previewHumanId) or the Preview ZelfProof guide linked above.
humanidDecrypt.paramFaceBase64Desc: Face image as raw base64 (this demo uses the same format as other biometric demos)
humanidDecrypt.paramOsDesc: DESKTOP, IOS, or ANDROID (demo uses DESKTOP)
humanidDecrypt.paramZelfProofDesc: HumanID string from paste, or from QR image extraction in this form
humanidDecrypt.paramPasswordDesc: Only if the proof was created with a password
humanidDecrypt.paramLivenessLevelDesc: When set, aligns with server liveness expectations for decrypt (see official guide)
humanidDecrypt.paramVerifierKeyDesc: If the proof was created with a verifier key
humanidDecrypt.paramQrCodeDesc: Image as a data URL (data:image/png;base64,...) or another form accepted by the API (this demo builds a data URL from the file)
humanidDecrypt.paramVerifierKeyQrDesc: Same semantics as on decrypt when the proof uses a verifier key

humanidPreview.heroSubtitle: Inspect public metadata from a ZelfProof. No face or credentials needed.
humanidPreview.qrUploadHelp: Upload a PNG or JPEG of the HumanID QR. We decode the QR and fill the proof string for preview.
humanidPreview.errorReadProof: Could not read HumanID from QR preview response.
humanidPreview.qrExtractSuccess: HumanID extracted from QR. Add a verifier key if needed, then preview.
humanidPreview.verifierKeyPlaceholder: Unlocks additional fields if set during creation

humanidPreview.apiPreviewEndpointDesc: Returns non-sensitive preview fields for a proof (for example whether a password is required) inside the usual signed data envelope.

humanidPreview.apiBullet1: This demo uses previewHumanId and, for QR images, previewZelfIdQr from @humanauthn/api-client with your session token.

humanidPreview.paramQrCodeDesc: Image as a data URL (data:image/png;base64,...) or another form accepted by the API (this demo builds a data URL from the file)
humanidPreview.paramVerifierKeyQrDesc: Same semantics as on preview when the proof uses a verifier key
